name: Nashville Money Guys
description_short: Nashville Money Guys is a hard money lender specializing in fix-and-flip and bridge loans for real estate investors, offering fast closings in 10 days or less with creative funding solutions.
description_long:
Nashville Money Guys was founded by three experienced real estate investors—Jason Dodge, Brandon Rumbley, and Brandon McDonald—who partnered in 2009 after building successful track records in real estate development and investment. Jason manages over $16 million in assets as CFO of Urban Dwell Homes; Brandon Rumbley has completed hundreds of deals and specializes in tax-advantaged strategies; and Brandon McDonald is a licensed general contractor with nearly 300 deals completed. The company positions itself as a private hard money lender rather than a traditional bank, targeting real estate investors who need quick capital for time-sensitive deals.

Nashville Money Guys offers two primary loan programs: Fix and Flip Loans for property rehabilitation and resale, and Bridge Loans for interim financing needs. They advertise fast funding (10 days or less), straightforward applications with minimal paperwork, competitive rates, and the ability to structure creative solutions including non-recourse lending for IRAs and lease-option arrangements. Their website includes a mortgage calculator and pre-approval letter tools. The company serves investors across Tennessee and Georgia, with testimonials mentioning loans for properties in bankruptcy, title disputes, and off-market deals.

What distinguishes Nashville Money Guys is the deep operational experience of its founders in real estate investing itself—they are not just lenders but active real estate operators who manage hundreds of rental units and complete 15-20 new construction builds annually. This background allows them to structure non-standard deals, work through title complications, and understand investor tax strategies. Testimonials highlight their willingness to extend loans when circumstances change and craft deal structures (like keeping owner financing in place) to reduce borrower costs. The company emphasizes transparency and positioning as "long-term financial partners" rather than transactional lenders.

However, hard money loans typically carry higher interest rates and fees than traditional bank financing, shorter repayment terms, and require substantial equity in the property. The website does not disclose specific rates, fees, loan terms, or maximum loan amounts. This is a specialized lending product designed for real estate professionals, not consumers seeking personal or small-business financing. Borrowers should expect rates and terms significantly less favorable than conventional mortgages or SBA loans, and this is suitable only for investors with real estate expertise and deals that justify the premium cost of speed and flexibility.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Founders are active real estate investors with 300+ combined deals and $16M+ in managed assets, providing credibility and operational insight
Advertises 10-day or faster closing timelines, critical for competitive real estate markets
Willingness to structure creative solutions including non-recourse IRA lending and lease-option arrangements
Can work with non-standard situations including properties in bankruptcy, title disputes, and off-market deals
Minimal application paperwork compared to traditional banks
Will extend loans and work through complications (per testimonials) rather than rigid underwriting
Local Nashville/Tennessee focus with established relationships and property management infrastructure
cons: Hard money lender with higher rates and fees than conventional financing—website does not disclose specific rates or terms
No information on loan amounts, maximum LTV, required equity, or actual approval timeline variability
Designed exclusively for real estate investors; not suitable for small business or consumer lending
Limited service area (primarily Tennessee/Georgia per testimonials) despite online application option
Testimonials mention deal complications (title disputes, bankruptcies) without clear disclosure of how common problem loans are
best_for: Real estate fix-and-flip investors needing quick capital in competitive markets
Investment property owners seeking bridge financing between sales or refinancing
Real estate investors with self-directed IRAs seeking non-recourse hard money loans
Investors with non-standard situations (bankruptcy, title issues, off-market deals) rejected by traditional lenders
diagnosis: Nashville Money Guys is best suited for experienced real estate investors seeking fast, flexible hard money financing for fix-and-flip or bridge loans. The main caveat is that hard money carries substantially higher costs (rates and fees not disclosed on website) than conventional financing and is appropriate only when speed and flexibility justify the premium—typically for time-sensitive or non-standard deals that traditional lenders won't approve. This is not a general-purpose business lender and unsuitable for most small business financing needs.
services: Fix and Flip Loans for property rehabilitation and resale
Bridge Loans for interim real estate financing
Non-recourse lending for self-directed IRA real estate investments
Creative deal structuring (lease-option, owner financing preservation)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Nashville Money Guys is a hard money lender specializing in fix-and-flip and bridge loans for real estate investors with fast closings.
